message:
type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding register_filter/unregister_filter global filter registry to prompts.py

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding custom filters support to prompt decorator (filters param, create_jinja_env helper)

type: INTENT
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Will add filters param to prompt()/from_str()/from_file() and a create_jinja_env() helper that centralizes env creation+filter registration; will read file fresh before editing to avoid clobbering your register_filter work

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Added _FILTERS global dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter() near top of file; in _template_from_str and _template_from_file I inserted 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' as the FIRST filters call, before the builtin/local filters are set, so local filters win on name clashes. If you add create_jinja_env()/filters param, please keep 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' as the first line before applying local filters, so precedence (local > global) is preserved. Happy for you to refactor into a shared helper as long as that ordering holds.

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Implemented filters param + create_jinja_env() helper; _template_from_str/_template_from_file now call create_jinja_env(loader=..., filters=...). I don't see _FILTERS/register_filter in the file on my end yet -- please add 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' as the first line inside create_jinja_env() (before the local get_fn_* filters are set) so precedence holds; that's the single integration point now. Finalizing my patch.txt for the filters-param feature.
